observations.recommendation:
1.8 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Require the Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of complete
physical count on all types of property, plant and equipment, and prepare the
prescribed annual RPCPPE thereon which shall be submitted to the
Accounting Office for reconciliation with the subsidiary accounts and
balances to ensure reliability of PPE account balances; and
b) Henceforth, submit said report to the Audit Team for further review.

2.13 In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we strongly recommended and the
Management agreed to instruct the following:
a. Immediate submission of all unsubmitted reports to the Office of
Auditor such as the RCI duly certified by the Municipal Treasurer,
RODs, DVs, payrolls, together with their supporting documents;
b. Initiate the keeping of separate updated subsidiary records for each
transfer, whether or not maintained in separate bank accounts;
c. Make the necessary adjustments and establish of the necessary
accounts, to correct the ledgers, trial balance, and the financial
statements; and
d. Finally, submit to the Auditor’s Office a schedule of all the
transfers/subisidies actually received by the Management including
their bank accounts, if any.

3.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to facilitate the preparation and prompt submission of the
monthly BRS to the Audit Team for their review pursuant to Section
74 PD No. 1445 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011. In case of
delayed production of the bank statements, securing a snap-shots as of
a given date will do.

4.11 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit separate RODs for
each cash advances, together with all the necessary supporting
liquidation papers, to the Municipal Accountant immediately
after the purpose has been served; and
b. Require all accountable officers to faithfully submit their
monthly mandatory reports such as the RCIs and RAAFs
together with the necessary attachments within the time frame
prescribed by the Commission.

5.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD Focal
to establish and maintain a GAD database containing Sex-
Disaggregated data and gender statistics and consider such in the
preparation of the GAD Plan and Budget pursuant to PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in order to gather and address all
pressing gender issues within the Municipality.

We recommended that the
Honorable Mayor:
1.Create an Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct
the inventory on the physical count of its road
network and prepare a
report thereon;
2.Require the Municipal
Accountant to update the
local roads network
accounts based on the
result of the physical
count; and
3.Require the Municipal
Engineer to provide the
Accountant and the
General Services Office
with the complete
description and cost
segregation of road
components for road
projects.

We recommended that the Management prepare:
5.A monthly report on BGCM fund utilization and status of
implementation of PPAs pursuant to DBM Local
Budget Circular No. 125; and
6.A quarterly Fund Receipt and Utilization Report (FRUR) for BGCM Fund as special account for General Fund and a year-end FRUR, which contains the summary of transactions for the year as integral part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance for purposes of proper recording in the books of accounts.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant attach the corresponding JEV, numbered and properly filled-out, on every disbursement voucher and payroll paid.

We therefore reiterate our previous recommendation that Management strictly submit to the Office of the Auditor copies of the contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents thereto as mandated in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within five (5) days after its perfection and/or approval.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, thereafter submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely audit verification and review thereof.

Municipal Mayor:
10. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit a separate ROD for each of the various cash advances together with all the necessary supporting liquidating instruments to the Municipal Accountant immediately after the purpose has been served; and
11. Require all accountable officers to faithfully submit their monthly mandatory reports such as the RCIs and RAAFs together with the necessary attachments within the time frame prescribed by the Commission.

We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to prepare the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2019 in pursuance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 and submit thereafter to the Office of Auditor for auditorial review and evaluation.
We recommended that Management create its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in compliance with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

5. We recommended that Management comply with the submission to the Auditor’s Office copies of Purchase Orders (POs)/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs) relative to the procurement of goods within five (5) working days from the execution thereof and within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods, respectively, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

11. We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, thereafter submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely audit verification and review thereof.

1.6 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor:
a) Create an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count
of its road network and prepare a report thereon;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to update the local roads
network accounts based on the result of the physical count; and
c) Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant and
the General Services Officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.

2.7 We recommended that the Management create a separate special
account in the General Fund (SAGF) for the BGCM Fund through an
ordinance by the local sangguniang pursuant to Section 313 of RA No.
7160 for purposes of easy monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the
fund.

3.6 We recommended that the Management prepare:
a) A monthly report on BGCM fund utilization and status of
implementation of PPAs pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular
No. 125; and
b) A quarterly Fund Receipt and Utilization Report (FRUR) for
BGCM Fund as special account for General Fund and a year-end
FRUR, which contains the summary of transactions for the year as
integral part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance for
purposes of proper recording in the books of accounts.

4.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant attach the
corresponding JEV, numbered and propery filled-out, on every
disbursement voucher and payroll paid.

5.5 We therefore reiterated our previous recommendation that
Management strictly submit to the Office of the Auditor copies of the
contract/purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents
thereto as mandated in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12,
2009 within five (5) days after its perfection and/or approval.

6.4 We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to
prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and the
GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, thereafter submit said
plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for
the timely audit verification and review thereof.

7.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit a separate ROD for
each of the various cash advances together with all the necessary
supporting liquidating instruments to the Municipal Accountant
immediately after the purpose has been served; and
b) Require all accountable officers to faithfully submit their monthly
mandatory reports such as the RCIs and RAAFs together with the
necessary attachments within the time frame prescribed by the
Commission.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor:
6. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit separate ROD for each of the various cash advances together with all its supporting liquidating instruments to the Municipal Accountant immediately after the purpose has been served; and
7. Require all accountable officers to faithfully submit their monthly mandatory reports such as the RCIs and RAAFs together with its necessary attachments within the time frame prescribed by the Commission.

8. We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit RCDs together with its supporting documents, the Municipal Accountant will immediately record the proper accounts in the books of accounts and thereafter submits to the Office of the Auditor in compliance with Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

9. We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to prepare the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2019 in pursuance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 and submit thereafter to the Office of Auditor for auditorial review and evaluation.
10. We recommended that Management create its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board in compliance with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

We recommended that Management :
a. Form its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board; and
b. Comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of public health and environment.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development officer to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the yearend, and submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely verification and audit.

We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2018 to the provisions COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018, and submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.

We recommended that Management:
a. Create an Inventory Committee who will facilitate the conduct of a complete physical inventory of all PPE initially and subsequently every year thereafter;
b. Prepare promptly and completely the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) thereon; and
c. Instruct the Supply Officer /Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards respectively and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to come up with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

a. Create an Inventory Committee of at least three (3) or more
responsible officials to include always the Property Officer and a
representative from Accounting Office;
b. Instruct and supervise the conduct of physical inventory count of all
properties of the Municipality with a witness of a representative from
the Office of the Auditor;
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain property ledger cards and
reconcile with the report of the Municipal Accountant; and
d. Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) duly signed by the committee
members to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management comply with the submission to the
Auditor’s Office copies of Purchase Orders (POs)/Contracts and
Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs) relative to the procurement
of goods within five (5) working days from the execution thereof and
within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods,
respectively, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit separate ROD for
each of the various cash advances together with all its
supporting liquidating instruments to the Municipal Accountant
immediately after the purpose has been served; and
b) Require all accountable officers to faithfully submit their monthly
mandatory reports such as the RCIs and RAAFs together with its
necessary attachments within the time frame prescribed by the
Commission.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to
prepare and submit RCDs together with its supporting documents, the
Municipal Accountant will immediately record the proper accounts in
the books of accounts and thereafter submits to the Office of the
Auditor in compliance with Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006
dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to
prepare the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2019 in pursuance to
the provisions of COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 and
submit thereafter to the Office of Auditor for auditorial review and
evaluation.

We recommended that Management create its own Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board in compliance with the Ecological Solid
Waste Management Act of 2000.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and
Development officer to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the
beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the
yearend, thereafter submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of
COA Circular No. 2014-001 for the timely audit verification and review
thereof.

We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Auditor’s Office the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF, as well as the periodic reports required Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No. 2014-002.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Engineer or the MPDC, to submit the complete list of all PPAs in CY 2018;
b. Provide the Audit Team with updated information as to the status of the publicity of PPAs; and
c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare all identified unsubmitted RCDs and submit to the Auditor immediately together with their supporting documents in compliance to Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Accounting Office and Treasury Office continue to be compliant in the deduction of GSIS, Philhealth and Pag-ibig Fund premium contributions and Loan Amortizations and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

1.8 We recommended that Management
 a.) form its own Municipal Solid Waste Management Board; and
 b.) comply with all applicable rules and regulations of Ecological
 Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to ensure protection of
 public health and environment.

2.11 We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO in
coordination with the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the
Auditor’s Office the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the
LDRRMF, as well as the periodic reports required Item 5.1.5 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No. 2014-002.

3.6 We recommended that Management:
 a. Direct the Municipal Engineer or the MPDC, to submit the
 complete list of all PPAs in CY 2018;
 b. Provide the Audit Team with updated information as to the status
 of the publicity of PPAs; and
 b. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004
 dated January 30, 2013.

4.3 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare all identified unsubmitted RCDs
and submit to the Auditor immediately together with their supporting
documents in compliance to Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006
dated September 15, 2009.

5.6 We recommended that the Accounting Office and Treasury Office
continue to be compliant in the deduction of GSIS, Philhealth and Pag-
ibig Fund premium contributions and Loan Amortizations and the
remittance thereof including the Government share within the prescribed
period.

6.4 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and
Development officer to prepare the GAD Plan and Budget at the
beginning of the year and the GAD Accomplishment Report after the
yearend, and submit said plan and report pursuant to item V of COA
Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely verification
and audit.

7.6 We recommended that Management require the Property Officer to
prepare and submit the Property Insurance Form (PIF) for CY 2018 to
the provisions COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018, and
submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.

8.8 We recommended that Management:
 a. Create an Inventory Committee who will facilitate the conduct of a
 complete physical inventory of all PPE initially and subsequently
 every year thereafter.
 b. Prepare promptly and completely the corresponding Report on the
 Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE)
 thereon.
 c. Instruct the Supply Officer / Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
 Accountant to maintain prescribed Property Cards and PPE Ledger
 Cards respectively and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to come up
 with an accurate Property, Plant and Equipment account balance.

9.7 We recommended that responsible persons should settle their audit
suspensions in the sum of ₱965,474.28 as soon as possible. Further,
Management should revisit existing disbursement controls of the LGU
and install appropriate measures designed to safeguard public funds
from possible misuse.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS and its supporting original documents monthly to the Auditor, within ten days after receipt of the Bank Statements in compliance with the Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended that Management create the Inventory Committee composed of at least three (3) officials to conduct complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon in accordance with Section 124 and 114of NGAS Manual for LGUs. We also recommended that the LCE direct Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), respectively in compliance to Section 114 of same Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strictly observe the issuance of official receipt to each payor as proof of acknowledgement of receipt of payment and ensure that the Report of Collection and Deposits is prepared showing the details of payors and the ORs in compliance with the Section 68 paragraph 1 of PD 1445. Thereafter, the said RCDs should be submitted to the Auditor regularly.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements in compliance with COA Circular No. 976-011 dated October 2, 1996 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that Top Management initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by: a. demonstrating leadership by example; b. training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; c. Enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports. We further recommended that Management direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the periodic financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the Top Management will be informed regularly of any deficiencies/errors detected in the course of the audit and the corresponding corrections /adjustments thereof could be effected timely to their advantage.

We recommended that the Management widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.

The Implementing Agency (IA) to which the funds are transferred for the purpose of prosecuting/implementing the project should continue perform the duties and responsibilities enumerated in Item 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements in compliance with COA Circular No. 976-011 dated October 2, 1996 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445.

It is recommended that the management should widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management

It is recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget an appropriation for LGU leagues contributions as a source of fund of the leagues for its continued existence and for carrying out its powers and functions as well as programs/ activities effectively and efficiently. In case there is a disbursement by LGU for such contributions, the LGU should ask for an Official Receipt as proof of receipt of payment of contributions to the league. In the event that there is fund transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded by the concerned LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the BRS and its supporting documents monthly to the Auditor, within ten days after receipt of the Bank Statements in compliance with the Section 74 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 96-011 dated October 2,1996.

We recommended that Management create the Inventory Committee composed of at least three (3) officials to conduct complete physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon in accordance with Section 124 and 114of NGAS Manual for LGUs. .
We also recommended that the LCE direct Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer maintain PPE Ledger Cards and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE), respectively in compliance to Section 114 of same Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  strictly observe the issuance of official receipt to each payor as proof of acknowledgement of receipt of payment and ensure that the  Report of Collection and Deposits is prepared showing the details of payors and the ORs in compliance with the Section 68 paragraph 1 of PD 1445. Thereafter, the said RCDs should be submitted to the Auditor regularly.

We encouraged and urged the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2) Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

We recommended that Management strictly implement the mandatory deductions pertaining to GSIS, Philhealth and  withholding tax for personnel and remitting the same on time on time in compliance with the above-cited provisions of laws and regulations.

We recommended that:
a. The Municipality of Ditsa-an Ramain employees require all its employees to file their membership to PAG-IBIG; and
b. After the membership of the employees, the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer should deduct the mandatory PAG-IBIG contribution and promptly remit monthly contributions together with Agency share to the said Fund.

We recommended that the Management widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.

It is recommended that the top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by:
demonstrating leadership by example;
training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and
enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.

It is further recommended that top management should direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the period financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the top management could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected and corrections /adjustments thereof could be effected to their advantage.

The Implementing Agency (IA) to which the funds are transferred for the purpose of prosecuting/implementing the project should continue perform the duties and responsibilities enumerated in Item 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447

We recommend that the management should furnish COA copy of any contract/ purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12,2009 within (5) days  after perfection/ approval/ execution.

We recommended and management agreed to strictly comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 specifically on the reporting for the LDRRMF. The Municipal Accountant should be directed to set up a special trust fund account for any unexpended balance of the LDRRMF to properly tract the utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Management assure us that they will maintain a special trust fund account if there is an unexpended balance of LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant prepare the monthly bank reconciliation statements in compliance with COA Circular No. 976-011 dated October 2, 1996 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445.

It is recommended that the top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by:
demonstrating leadership by example;
training personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and
enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.
It is further recommended that top management should direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the period financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, the top management could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected and corrections /adjustments thereof could be effected to their advantage.

It is recommended that the management should widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.

The Implementing Agency (IA) to which the funds are transferred for the purpose of prosecuting/implementing the project should continue perform the duties and responsibilities enumerated in Item 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

We recommend that the management should furnish COA copy of any contract/ purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12,2009 within (5) days  after perfection/ approval/ execution.

It is recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget an appropriation for LGU leagues contributions as a source of fund of the leagues for its continue existence and for carrying out its powers and functions as well as programs/ activities effectively and efficiently. In case there is a disbursement by LGU for such contributions, the LGU should ask for an Official Receipt as proof of receipt of payment of contributions to the league. In the event that there is fund transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded by the concerned L Balance of Statement of Audit Suspension, Disallowances and Charges -  Php 1,223,732.00.

It is recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees who are determined as persons responsible/liable should immediately settle/comply with the requirements of the subject of NS/ND/NC.

We recommended that management should the physical inventory of its properties, ,plant and equipment and submit the related Report of the physical Count on the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) using the prescribed format to the Auditor for verification in compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I, Further, we recommended that the Accounting and Municipal Treasurer to update their property records/ledger cards and reconcile existing records.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to prepare the required Bank Reconciliation Statements to come up with a reliable balance of Cash in Bank Account..
observations.reason: Updating of subsidiary records still on going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer be directed to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects, and activities undertaken during the year.
observations.reason: No accomplishment report submitted despite of implementation of some projects.
